page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7ne.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with a dedicated Intel Xeon 8369HB processor running at 3.3 GHz. The configuration includes 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs, with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance, and network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals average single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling. The stress-ng single-core CPU test scores 1572.49, and single-threaded gzip compression reaches 27.19 GB/sec. However, multi-core stress-ng performance drops to 2070.9, placing it in the bottom 10% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with read-only large block bandwidth at 16.29 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 14.38 GB/sec. Passmark CPU and memory scores are similarly low, indicating that the instance is best suited for workloads that do not demand high parallel processing or intensive memory throughput.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.g7ne.large offers a dedicated CPU allocation at an entry-level resource density, making it a predictable option for lightweight workloads compared to shared-core burstable instances. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and moderate single-core capabilities, this instance is suitable for basic web serving, lightweight background tasks, single-threaded applications, and small-scale development or testing environments that require dedicated CPU execution without high memory or storage demands.

Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1572.49 and solid single-threaded compression speeds. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the lower percentiles, with a multi-core stress-ng score of 2070.9. Qualitatively, the dedicated CPU allocation offers predictable execution for entry-level workloads. This instance is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded applications, and basic development or testing environments.
